content: Syllables have a minimum of one structural part (as in the word air /eə/), and a maximum of _______ structural parts (as in the word texts /teksts/).
__________ consonants are consonants which form the nucleus of a syllable that does not contain a vowel.
The V and CV syllables are referred to as a/an ______ syllable because they end with a vowel.
The longest possible initial consonant cluster may have ______ consonants in front of the vowel.
The longest possible final consonant cluster may have ______ consonants after the vowel.
_______ stress is the relative degree of force given to a certain syllable in a word of more than one syllable when it is pronounced in isolation, as if quoted from a dictionary.
With _____ clusters of three consonants, the first sound is always /s/, the second sound is a voiceless plosive /p, t, k/, and the third sound is one of the four approximants /l, r, w, j/.

Syllabically, although a diphthong is a combination of two vowel sounds and is marked by a glide from the first vowel to the second, it is perceived as one phoneme, not two. Thus, diphthongs are treated as having one _____ only.
Which of the following has the right stress placement for “White House” in the “The president of the United States lives in the White House”?
Which of the following words has the stress on the second syllable?
Which of the following has/have the right stress placement?
Which of the following is an open-syllable word?
Which of the following describe(s) a stressed syllable correctly?
Which of the following are disyllabic words?
In which cases is the stress usually located on the root or stem of the word?
options: A. Prefixes usually do not affect the placement of the stress.
B. Suffixes may affect the placement of the stress.
C. Compound words usually stress the second component.
D. Monosyllabic words have stress on the only syllable.

content: Syllables are units of language, smaller than a word but larger than a phoneme.
The first part of a syllable of the CVC pattern is called the onset... The combination of the nucleus and coda is called the rhyme.
Word stress in English is not as predictable as it is in other languages...
Not all English vowel phonemes can occur in the CV syllable pattern, but all can appear in the CVC pattern.
English has a complex system of consonant clusters...
In polysyllabic words, if the primary stress falls on the third syllable or later, the secondary stress is usually marked...
Monosyllabic words cannot be split into parts and written in two lines.
Some words in English are shown in dictionaries as having three levels of stress: primary, secondary, unstressed.
The major stress on two-syllable words is more likely to fall on the second syllable if the word is a noun or an adjective, and on the first syllable if the word is a verb.
Every language is said to have syllables.
